AC_lambda.AC_lambda_closure_status: rigorously closed via interval-certified Kawamoto-Smit block-diagonality
AC_lambda.claim: Free fermion propagator block-diagonal on hw=1 corner basis (species-label decomposition)
AC_lambda.kawamoto_smit_reference: Kawamoto, N. & Smit, J. (1981). Effective Lagrangian and dynamical symmetry breaking in strongly coupled lattice QCD. Nucl. Phys. B192, 100-124.

AC_phi.AC_phi_no_go_status: RECLASSIFIED FROM 'OPEN OBSTRUCTION' TO 'STRUCTURAL NO-GO WITHIN A_MIN'. The C_3-preserved interpretation note (2026-05-08) retires the 'C_3 must be broken' framing; C_3[111] is a load-bearing preserved symmetry. The equal-expectation lemma is the framework's structural prediction, not an admitted obstruction.
AC_phi.claim: Every C_3[111]-symmetric self-adjoint operator on H_{hw=1} has equal expectation Tr(H)/3 on every corner-basis state

AC_phi_lambda.AC_phi_lambda_closure_status: REFRAMED FROM 'OPEN DERIVATION RESIDUAL' TO 'LABELING-CONVENTION BRIDGE'. Parameter-counting on C_3-equivariant 3-orbit bijections forces the 3-fold count and reduces the residual to a global cyclic relabeling, identical in nature to standard particle-physics conventions {u, c, t}, {nu_1, nu_2, nu_3}, etc. Full positive derivation closure still requires either an explicit user-approved labeling axiom or C_3-breaking dynamics; per the C_3-preserved interpretation note the framework's stance is the labeling-convention bridge, not C_3-breaking.
AC_phi_lambda.claim: Identification of framework hw=1 3-fold structure with SM flavor-generation structure
labeling_convention_examples: {u, c, t}: SM up-type mass-ordering convention
AC_phi_lambda.method: parameter-counting on C_3-equivariant 3-orbit bijections + labeling-convention reframe
AC_phi_lambda.remaining_open_residual: Strict 'derivation' closure of which cyclic shift maps hw=1 corner -> SM generation requires either (a) explicit user-approved labeling axiom or (b) C_3-breaking dynamics; neither is added here.
